title: Mega Banette Battle Guide
summary: A [Ghost] attacker–disruptor hybrid — [prankster] makes [will-o-wisp] and [taunt] priority, and 165 Attack turns [shadow-claw] into a real threat.
strengths: 165 Attack is one of the highest among Mega Pokemon
weaknesses: No Speed boost tools and base 75 Speed

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Banette is a rare hybrid of offensive power and utility — [prankster] gives priority to its status moves, and 165 Attack lets [shadow-claw] deal real damage. The result is a disruptor that can simultaneously cripple physical attackers with [will-o-wisp], lock out setup sweepers with [taunt], and still threaten direct KOs.

## Strengths
[prankster] priority on [will-o-wisp] and [taunt] is the defining ability interaction — Mega Banette disables the opposing team before any damage is traded. 165 Attack puts [shadow-claw] in the upper tier of [Ghost] STABs, and the [Ghost] typing grants immunity to [Normal] and [Fighting] (including [fake-out]).

## Weaknesses & Answers
Bulk of 64/75/83 is very fragile — priority moves or super-effective hits will one-shot it. [Dark] and [Ghost] attackers are the main threats. Speed 75 without any boost tools means Mega Banette relies on [prankster] or [sucker-punch] for tempo, not raw speed.

## Recommended Moveset
[shadow-claw] is the main STAB. [will-o-wisp] is priority burn thanks to [prankster]. [taunt] is priority [taunt] that shuts down setup leads and stall pivots. [sucker-punch] closes the set as a priority finisher.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[banettite] is mandatory. [prankster] activates only after Mega Evolution. Tera [Ghost] stacks STAB for stronger [shadow-claw] damage, while Tera [Fairy] flips the [Dark] and [Ghost] weaknesses into resistances.

## Teammates & Synergy
[intimidate] support covers the poor physical bulk. [fake-out] users like [incineroar] buy safe turns. [Fairy] partners punish the [Dark]/[Ghost] weakness.

## Play Patterns
Mega evolve and immediately [prankster]-burn the most threatening physical attacker, or [taunt] a setup lead. Follow with [shadow-claw] for direct damage and [sucker-punch] to clean up faster attackers. Be cautious with switch-ins — Mega Banette cannot eat super-effective hits.
